programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team head for the Vegan Aisle for all the news on animal-free food. Helen Lawal visits Tesco to find out how they keep up with all the plant-based burger trend, John Whaite seeks out the secret to the snap in a vegan sausage skin, Kate Quilton finds out how exactly you milk an oat, and Briony May Williams learns how to make a quiche without egg

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This weeks competition is in and around Leeds, where the first host, 38-year-old foster carer and nature lover Shaun, is serving up an Asia-meets-Yorkshire fusion menu complete with dumplings, Chinese fish and chips and a not-so-traditional Yorkshire pudding

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring model Ryan is hoping that his French and Caribbean cuisine will tantalise his guests tastebuds in Leeds. During the drinks reception, Ryan is fuming when one guest flouts his runway-ready dress code, and at the table, Ryan bites back with his egg salad bombs then insults another diner when they comment on his clobber. Desperate to lighten the mood, he hosts a modelling masterclass, but his controversial views on beauty backfire. Having spent the evening insulting and then bickering with his guests, has Ryan blown his chances to bag the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On night three in Leeds, medical trainer Jen hosts a pink-themed party in an attempt to bag the big bucks. Despite her overuse of pink food colouring, her guests are impressed with her home-cooked dishes, but when Shaun questions her on their provenance, shes forced to come clean. Philip teases the table with his hidden pink side and Ryan isnt afraid to throw some insults when Philip reveals that he won a clothing competition. A surprise performance in the garden proves a cut above for hairdresser Sarah, but will it be enough for Jen to grab the grand?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the penultimate night in Leeds, a Middle Eastern mezze is the suggested offering from freelance creative Philip. Shaun is impressed with his homemade sourdough, but a cheesecake doesnt cut it for hairdresser Sarah. The group gets creative with some clay modelling, but it all goes pear-shaped when Ryan continues to throw insults at his fellow guests. Jen entertains the table with a bizarre cleaning story and Philip ventures into the woods for a muddy mis-endeavour

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Leeds, hair salon owner Sarah hopes to delight her diners with some classic 1980s cuisine. With a reputation for being outspoken, Sarah gets a dose of her own medicine from one diner whos determined to get their own back. Later, a guest reveals a freaky phobia and theres a hair-raising horror story before some very fancy footwork on the dancefloor. Will an evening of disco dancing and retro grub be enough for Sarah to take top spot?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chef Mark and yoga teacher Loretta, who live in west London, have been together for 15 years after meeting in Lorettas native Australia. programme.desc: Part one of two. Cameras follow six well-known faces as they step back in time to explore Irelands past in deeply personal ways. Trade Unionist Mick Lynch, Raidio na Gaeltachta presenter Gormfhlaith Ní Thuairisg and architect Dermot Bannon explore their history through the 1926 Census

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: Kildare the zebra is still struggling to stand up after being under anaesthetic and the team face race against time to save the elderly animal. Jasmina the red panda undergoes a quick dental procedure and finds a new male in her habitat when she returns, while Tundra the Amur tiger receives a contraceptive chip

programme.title: To Catch a Thief (1955)
programme.desc: A retired cat burglar becomes the prime suspect for a series of robberies carried out in his own distinctive style, and sets out to clear his name by catching the copycat crook responsible. His quest takes him to the Cote dAzur, where he encounters the demure daughter of a wealthy holiday-maker, and before long the two are drawn together. Alfred Hitchcocks romantic mystery, starring Cary Grant, Grace Kelly and Jessie Royce Landis

programme.title: Nuacht
programme.desc: The latest news headlines

programme.title: RTÉ News
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: The Angelus
programme.sub-title: Field of Dreams, Curraheen, Cork - Saturday
programme.desc: Young adults with Downs syndrome learn to plant, grow, harvest and sell produce in their own Field of Dreams market garden in Curraheen, County Cork

programme.desc: The latest national and international headlines

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Pat Shortts Entertainment from DTelly
programme.desc: Pat looks back on a sizzling selection of famous voices comprising leading comic actors, impressionists and impersonators from the RTE Archive, as Peter Ustinov, Ron Moody, Oliver Callan, Michael Caine and Niall Toibin make a show of themselves by doing their individual take on other well-known showbiz folk. Stand-up, both new and not so new, also features

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Sun and sand are the new dream, but the lads cannot afford it, until Billy Murphy turns up with a daring heist idea. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy, Chris Walley and Shane Casey

programme.title: The Tommy Tiernan Show
programme.desc: Improvised chat show in which neither the presenter nor the viewers are told the identity of the guests in advance. With the comedian in the dark about who he will be interviewing until they walk out to greet him, anything can happen - especially if Tommy doesnt have a clue who they are

programme.title: Soda Blonde - Straight Into the Arms of Something
programme.desc: The alt-pop band perform at Dublins National Concert Hall in 2025, accompanied by the 60-piece National Symphony Orchestra

programme.title: The Week in Politics
programme.desc: Reflections on the political week, including contributions from journalists, senators and TDs. Hosted by Áine Lawlor

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A young man sat alone in the church and covered in blood confesses to Sidney and Geordie that he attacked and killed his landlord. However, when the pair investigate further, they find the alleged victim alive and well with his family. Elsewhere, Margarets visits to the vicarage begin to leave Mrs Maguire irritated, Leonard is distracted by secretive trips to the cinema, and Geordie is compelled to deal with a surprising shoplifter. Drama set in the 1950s, starring James Norton and Robson Green

programme.title: Nationwide

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Boniface is called by DI Gillespie to the home of minister of defence Charles Streatham, where guest Mary Sparkes has been found dead at the bottom of a staircase. Quickly assessing Sparkes has been murdered by poisoning rather than by a fall, Gillespie and DS Livingstone have to identify the murderer among the persons in the house - one is reporter Ruth Penny, disguised as a maid and looking for a scoop

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries
programme.desc: Frankie concocts a chilling ruse to expose a conman who claims he can speak to the dead. Period crime drama, starring Lauren Lee Smith as the first female private detective in 1920s Toronto

programme.desc: Designer Emily Moorcroft sets out to create a sanctuary space and a garden that will water itself, hoping it will secure her place at Bord Bia Bloom. Emily used to be a detective in the London Metropolitan Police. Her husband was offered a job in Germany, so she stepped back from police work. When they returned to Dublin, Emily decided not to go back to the Met and transitioned into garden design

programme.title: Irelands Coast
programme.desc: Exploring the people of Irelands ancient relationship with the waters that surround the island and examining the connections that the population have had with the coastal environments over time

programme.title: Come to Your Census
programme.desc: Part two of two. In Dublins Liberties, author Joseph OConnor traces his familys working-class roots, uncovering a community that shaped several generations of his family. Actor Eileen Walsh is in Cork to discover the story of her grandfather, a young revolutionary. In Mayo, radio presenter Louise Duffy explores the powerful roles played by women in the rural community where she grew up

programme.title: The Money List

programme.title: Fair City

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine OClock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Jack Reacher (2012)
programme.desc: A former army sniper is arrested following a random shooting that leaves five people dead, but protests his innocence. An elusive former military police investigator is called in to examine the case, and becomes convinced a conspiracy is at work. Action thriller based on Lee Childs novel One Shot, starring Tom Cruise, Rosamund Pike, Werner Herzog and Robert Duvall

programme.title: The Gone
programme.desc: As the team investigates and tries to find their latest suspect, Mervin, they become convinced someone else out there is pulling the strings. Aileen reconciles with Richter, spurring him to take some advice from Dr Ray about his Huntingtons disease. Plus, Judge Hannah Martin finally gets through to her daughter Sinead, as they both confront their past traumas

programme.title: The Tourist
programme.desc: Elliot returns to the hotel to find Helen missing, but Ethan is on hand to help retrace her steps. Helen must balance the peril she now finds herself in, with her desire to get the answers she needs. The McDonnells hunt for Elliot gathers pace, thanks to an old friend who takes their side. Meanwhile, Niamh reluctantly agrees to go into hiding in a place haunted with old memories

programme.title: Hypnotic (2023)
programme.desc: Police detective Danny Rourke is haunted by the abduction of his seven-year-old daughter Minnie from a park. During a bank heist, Danny discovers a photo of his daughter with an instruction to find someone called Dell Rayne. Evidence leads to a psychic Diana Cruz, who introduces the cop to the unseen world of hypnotics - people with the ability to control how we perceive the world. Action thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Alice Braga, William Fichtner and JD Pardo

programme.title: The Rumour
programme.desc: Joanna is blindsided when a reporter questions her about the chilling rumour - someone has fed him information. Bea urges her to drop it, but threats force Joanna and Alfie to stay with her. Then she discovers Michael has picked up Alfie from school without her knowledge. Panic consumes her as she frantically searches, fearing the worst. As the press exposes Sonia, tensions explode. On Halloween, the kids enter her house after finding the door open - and inside, they make a shocking discovery

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith judge their efforts

But his doctor has an agenda of his own, which leads to all manner of complications. Comedy, starring Peter OToole, Peter Sellers, Woody Allen, Ursula Andress and Romy Schneider

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, writer and director, best known for his roles in The Producers, Blazing Saddles, Young Frankenstein and Willy Wonka and the Chocolate Factory

programme.title: Classic Movies: The Story Of
programme.desc: Ian Nathan examines more cinematic landmarks, beginning with David Leans 1946 adaptation of the novel by Charles Dickens, which starred John Mills as Pip and Valerie Hobson as Estella

programme.title: Making a Maestro
programme.desc: The winner of the Donatella Flick Conducting Competition is announced, with one of the 20 contenders getting the chance to become an LSO assistant conductor

programme.title: Pink Floyd: A Delicate Sound of Thunder
programme.desc: A concert film from the musicians A Momentary Lapse of Reason tour, recorded at New Yorks Nassau Coliseum in August 1988 using 27 cameras. The performance sees David Gilmour, Rick Wright and Nick Mason deliver seminal tracks including Shine on You Crazy Diamond, Time, Comfortably Numb and Wish You Were Here

programme.title: Pink Floyd - Live at Pompeii
programme.desc: A 1972 concert in which David Gilmour, Nick Mason, Roger Waters and Richard Wright perform hits including Echoes and A Saucerful of Secrets amid the empty ruins of Pompeiis Ancient Roman amphitheatre

programme.title: Have You Got It Yet? The Story of Syd Barrett and Pink Floyd
programme.desc: The life of the bands original frontman, charting his creative and destructive impulses, his breakdown and exit from the group and his subsequent life. Featuring interviews with Syds friends, lovers, family and bandmates Roger Waters, David Gilmour and Nick Mason

programme.title: The Who: Live at the Isle of Wight
programme.desc: The bands performance at the festival in August 1970, featuring hits I Cant Explain, My Generation and Magic Bus as well as tracks from their double album Tommy

programme.title: Greatest Basslines
programme.desc: Former Hole and Smashing Pumpkins bassist Melissa Auf der Maur deconstruct the basslines that defined her career, and shares the music that first turned her on to the instrument and shaped her playing style

programme.title: Down the Tracks: The Music That Influenced Bob Dylan
programme.desc: Records and artists that inspired the singer during his formative years, including Pete Seeger, Blind Willie McTell and Harry Smiths Anthology of American Folk Music

programme.title: Bob Dylan: The Other Side of the Mirror
programme.desc: Murray Lerners film documenting Bob Dylans appearances at the Newport Folk Festival in Rhode Island from 1963 to 1965, revealing a metamorphosis from folk prodigy to rocks fiercely confrontational poet

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music critics discuss the lives and careers of the members of the folk rock supergroup, which was made up of David Crosby, Stephen Stills, Graham Nash and Neil Young

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music critics examine the life and career of the Canadian singer-songwriter, who was a driving force behind some of the biggest groups of the 60s before finding success on his own

programme.title: Mozart: Le Nozze di Figaro
programme.desc: A performance of the opera

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: The fate of a newlywed seems sealed after he suffers a string of accidents similar to his wifes first husband

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Jack and Ednas parrot lays a strange egg, but when the chick hatches it appears to be watching them

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: I Like It Here in Wilmington
programme.desc: A troubled businessman contemplates murdering his wife so he can get his hands on her trust fund

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Two villains decide to take advantage of an old man

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: When a young couple move into a new neighbourhood, they find their lives and marriage threatened by the boy next door

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: A man attempts to stop his insensitive wife from alienating their family and friends. Drama, starring Joan Fontaine and Gary Merrill

programme.title: Making a Maestro
programme.desc: The winner of the Donatella Flick Conducting Competition is announced, with one of the 20 contenders getting the chance to become an LSO assistant conductor

programme.title: 42nd Street
programme.desc: A performance of the Broadway song and dance musical extravaganza, featuring the songs Were in the Money, Lullaby of Broadway and Dames

programme.title: The Highwaymen Live: American Outlaws
programme.desc: A 1990 concert at the Nassau Coliseum in Uniondale, New York, by the country supergroup comprising Willie Nelson, Waylon Jennings, Johnny Cash and Kris Kristofferson

programme.title: Liza Minnelli: Hollywoods Golden Child
programme.desc: A profile of the actress and singer, with archive footage and interviews revealing her triumphs, struggles with addiction and resilient spirit

programme.title: Chicago (2002)
programme.desc: Two female jailbirds imprisoned for murder pull out all the stops to seize the attention of the media. Aided by a slick lawyer, both women are desperate to find fame and fortune and escape the death penalty by winning public sympathy. Musical, starring Renee Zellweger, Catherine Zeta-Jones, Richard Gere, John C Reilly and Queen Latifah

programme.title: Dolly Parton: Song by Song
programme.desc: The Country and Western singer looks back at her most famous songs, discussing what inspired them, their impact and how they continue to influence people to this day. She begins with Coat of Many Colors, which tells the story of the jacket her mum made her from multicoloured rags - and the bullies it attracted at school. Including contributions from Hollywood actress Kristin Chenoweth and record producer Steve Buckingham

programme.title: Dolly Parton: Song by Song
programme.desc: The singer looks back at her early career and talks about the inspiration behind Jolene, one of her most famous hits, which has been covered by more than 400 artists

programme.title: Opry Live
programme.sub-title: Rhonda Vincent, Eddie Montgomery, Travelin McCourys, Chris Young, Trace Adkins, Gretchen Wilson
programme.desc: Rhonda Vincent, Eddie Montgomery, the Travelin McCourys, Chris Young, Trace Adkins and Gretchen Wilson take to the stage of world-famous Nashville music venue the Grand Ole Opry

programme.title: National Theatre Live: Much Ado About Nothing
programme.desc: When a hotel owners daughter weds a soldier, not all guests are in the mood for love. A 2022 performance of Shakespeares comedy, starring Katherine Parkinson and John Heffernan

programme.title: Larry Graham and Graham Central Station
programme.desc: A 2010 concert by the pioneering funk bassist and his band at the Bataclan in Paris

programme.title: Record On
programme.desc: The stories behind classic albums, focusing on Paul Wellers journey through changing times in the 1990s, his new direction in sound and his resulting 1993 album Wild Wood

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: In the town of Ennis, Alaska, the eight men working at the Tsalal Arctic Research Station all disappear. Local police chief Liz Danvers leads the investigation, but state trooper Evangeline Navarro believes it may be linked to an unsolved murder. The return of the crime anthology, starring Jodie Foster and Kali Reis

programme.desc: Sofia works to secure her familys strength, while Oz attempts to play both the Falcone and Maroni families to his advantage

programme.title: Fish Town

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Maritime Inn landlady Pat gets her first full house of the season as Brixhams tourist trade picks up for the summer - but refuses to hire anyone to help her with the extra work. Powerboat instructor Mike Weatherbee tries to enforce the laws of the sea, carpet-cleaner Dave Satchwill prepares to lead the towns popular ghost tour, and lone fisherman Dave Brown reveals his unusual methods

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: August arrives and the fishermen of Brixham spend time ashore with their families as their boats are repaired - but summer proves costly for Jan, who loses £1,000 for every day his vessel is out of action. Apprentice deckhand Logan has to decide whether a life at sea is the right choice for him and his family, and seafood seller Jenny receives a surprise on her 65th birthday

programme.title: Atomic
programme.desc: In Beirut, deep cover CIA agent Cassie Elliott receives a tip off about an exchange of uranium that has apparently just taken place in the city, close to where Max and JJ are now laying low, and decides to investigate. Meanwhile, Max receives evidence that the cartel has kidnapped his girlfriend Laetitia and he must take the statues all the way to Marrakech if he is to save her

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street

programme.desc: Kellerman and Lewis are ordered to Pennsylvania to extradite a female murder suspect. Police drama, starring Clark Johnson and Reed Diamond

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. He begins with a fry-up breakfast before visiting the remains of a Roman amphitheatre and investigating a mysterious secret in a Liverpool Street hotel, as well as meeting former EastEnders star Larry Lamb (Archie Mitchell)

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capitals love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the citys first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The trial of Jack McCall continues as the jury retire to mull over the evidence they have heard. Elsewhere, the townsfolk lay the outlaw Wild Bill to rest, and Bullock decides to take the law into his own hands

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The smallpox virus threatens the very existence of the inhabitants of Deadwood, as it engulfs the entire town and leaves no one well enough to go and get any medicine. Meanwhile, Swearengen is still after Almas gold claim

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Two teenagers come to town with a tale that theyre in search of their runaway father, but when they see the two rival saloons the dollar signs ring up in their eyes and they decide to split up and each take a job at separate establishments. Meanwhile, tensions rise when Bullock returns with a fresh ultimatum for Swearengen

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The people of the town begin to latch on to what Miles and Flora are up to, and decide its time for someone to put a stop to their games. Meanwhile, a vaccine arrives which is to be administered for smallpox

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: In the wake of the death of his boss Carmine Falcone, Oz Cobb attempts to legitimise himself within Gothams criminal underworld. Crime drama spin-off from The Batman film, starring Colin Farrell

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: Sofia works to secure her familys strength, while Oz attempts to play both the Falcone and Maroni families to his advantage

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: On Wallach IX, young Valya Harkonnen promises Mother Superior Raquella that shell protect the Sisterhood by putting one of their own on the imperial throne. Thirty years later, on Salusa Secundus, Valya works to combat a new threat to her long-awaited plan for a marriage between Princess Ynez-Arat and a young Richese prince. Sci-fi prequel, starring Emily Watson, Olivia Williams and Mark Strong

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: After receiving word about events on Salusa Secundus, Valya brings Theodosia to the Imperial House to help manage the situation. Meanwhile, Desmond makes a confession to Emperor Corrino, Tula reluctantly enlists Lila for a vital mission, and distrust swirls around Keiran Atreides, the sword master for House Corrino

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: Following a tragedy, the young Tula worries about being accepted despite her family name, while a sceptical Valya struggles with the decision to take the Sisterhood vow. Years later, Valya receives a message that confirms her suspicions, and Tula mourns a loss

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: After nearly all the acolytes have the same nightmare, Tula attempts to understand the origins of their disturbing dreams. Meanwhile, on the eve of the great Landsraad meeting, Valya sets her plan in motion to redeem House Harkonnen, Ynez confronts her father about rumors surrounding Desmond, and Kieran conspires to advance a rebel attack that the Sisterhood must try to thwart

programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: As Corrino names a new Bashar, a Sister with deep connections to the emperor arrives on Salusa Secundus, sending ripples through the royal house. programme.title: Dune: Prophecy
programme.desc: As Tula contends with the stunning revelation of Desmonds true identity, the acolytes uncover a devastating long-held secret about the Sisterhood. Meanwhile, Valya executes her plan for Ynezs escape, which leads to an epic confrontation with an increasingly powerful Desmond

programme.title: Prisoner
programme.desc: Held captive at a secret weapons factory, Amber and Tibor narrowly escape execution by Nina before agreeing to a deal to help each other. Tibor leads Ambers husband Olly to a safe house, saving her family from Pegasus

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Rue is offered a risky deal that draws her deeper into Alamos world, while Nate battles the city council, and Maddy gives Cassie a mission that could launch her new career. Lexi enlists Jules to create an original painting for LA Nights

programme.title: Atomic
programme.desc: In Beirut, deep cover CIA agent Cassie Elliott receives a tip off about an exchange of uranium that has apparently just taken place in the city, close to where Max and JJ are now laying low, and decides to investigate. Meanwhile, Max receives evidence that the cartel has kidnapped his girlfriend Laetitia and he must take the statues all the way to Marrakech if he is to save her

programme.title: The Penguin
programme.desc: Sofia makes plans for a more hopeful future as she confronts the events that turned her into the Hangman and led to a decade-long fight to survive in Arkham State Hospital

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Egans wife is killed and his son is taken into protective custody, while Bayliss and Pembleton continue their investigation in New York. Police drama, starring Kyle Secor

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Heated Rivalry
programme.desc: Shane and Ilya keep their secret fling alive while their growing connection is tested by personal and professional pressures

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment (1985)
programme.desc: Americas most inept cops graduate and are let loose on the streets - under the command of an unscrupulous lieutenant whose promotion depends on their failure. Fortunately for him, their unique brand of policing actually leads to an increase in the crime rate. Comedy sequel, starring Steve Guttenberg, Bubba Smith, David Graf and Michael Winslow

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training (1986)
programme.desc: Facing closure from cutbacks in public spending and competition from a more successful academy, the police training school calls on its bungling graduates to act as instructors and make good officers out of a bunch of new recruits even more incompetent than they are. Comedy sequel, starring Steve Guttenberg, Bubba Smith, Michael Winslow and Art Metrano

programme.title: Footloose (1984)
programme.desc: A rebellious, hip-swinging student from the city moves to a small religious community, where he instantly clashes with an old-school fire-and-brimstone preacher - who has banned dancing and rock n roll because he considers them immoral. Drama, starring Kevin Bacon, Lori Singer, John Lithgow, Chris Penn, Dianne Wiest and Sarah Jessica Parker

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: The Breakfast Club (1985)
programme.desc: Five teenagers who occupy different areas of their schools social structure are thrown together for a Saturday morning detention. During the day, they analyse their behaviour and find unexpected common ground. Drama, directed by John Hughes and starring Emilio Estevez, Judd Nelson, Molly Ringwald, Anthony Michael Hall, Ally Sheedy and Paul Gleason

programme.title: Footloose (1984)
programme.desc: A rebellious, hip-swinging student from the city moves to a small religious community, where he instantly clashes with an old-school fire-and-brimstone preacher - who has banned dancing and rock n roll because he considers them immoral. Drama, starring Kevin Bacon, Lori Singer, John Lithgow, Chris Penn, Dianne Wiest and Sarah Jessica Parker

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman (1982)
programme.desc: A Navy recruit undergoing officer training has an uneasy romance with a factory worker. He also attracts the attention of a tough, uncompromising sergeant, who sees his potential and is determined to push him to the limit. Romantic drama, starring Richard Gere, Debra Winger, Louis Gossett Jr, Robert Loggia, David Keith and David Caruso

programme.title: Ghostbusters (1984)
programme.desc: Three eccentric scientists put their hi-tech gadgetry and knowledge of the paranormal to money-making use by setting up in business as freelance ghost catchers. A musician calls them in to investigate when she sees strange creatures in her kitchen, only to end up possessed. As the spook-hunting team members deal with an attempt to shut their operation down, they discover an evil demonic entity is being summoned into existence and it falls to them to stop this creature from destroying the world. Supernatural comedy adventure, starring Bill Murray, Dan Aykroyd, Harold Ramis, Sigourney Weaver, Ernie Hudson and Rick Moranis

programme.title: Ghostbusters II (1989)
programme.desc: The eccentric spectre-hunters reunite to save New York from a massive river of slime generated by the citizens negativity and nasty thoughts. Meanwhile, the spirit of a 16th-century tyrant tries to return to the land of the living by possessing the body of a toddler. Sequel to the supernatural comedy, starring Bill Murray, Sigourney Weaver, Dan Aykroyd, Harold Ramis, Rick Moranis and Ernie Hudson

programme.title: Batman (1989)
programme.desc: The masked avenger stalks the streets of Gotham City, waging a nightly war against the criminal element. However, he may have met his match when the citys crime syndicate is taken over by the crazed Joker, who plans to poison the populace with toxic chemicals. Tim Burtons comic-book adventure, starring Michael Keaton, Jack Nicholson and Kim Basinger

programme.title: Blade Runner (1982)
programme.desc: A former cop in a bleak future Los Angeles is forced to return to duty for a mission to hunt down and kill a group of renegade android replicants. The fugitives have escaped from a space colony to find their creator on Earth, in a bid to prolong their limited lifespan. Theatrical cut of Ridley Scotts sci-fi thriller, originally released in cinemas, starring Harrison Ford, Rutger Hauer, Sean Young and Daryl Hannah

programme.title: The Running Man (1987)
programme.desc: A helicopter pilot is framed for the murder of starving civilians - and ends up being forced to take part in a brutal TV game show in which a succession of gadget-wielding killers hunt him on the streets of Los Angeles. Sci-fi action adventure inspired by a Stephen King story, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Yaphet Kotto, Maria Conchita Alonso and Richard Dawson

programme.title: Mad Max 2 (1981)
programme.desc: A former cop haunted by his tragic past roams the wasteland of Australia in the aftermath of a nuclear war. He arrives at a besieged settlement and grudgingly helps protect the residents from a horde of marauding bikers who want to get hold of their valuable supply of petrol. Futuristic adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Bruce Spence, Vernon Wells, Emil Minty and Kjell Nilsson

programme.title: The Lost Boys (1987)
programme.desc: Two teenage brothers move with their mother to a coastal town in California, where the eldest falls in with a local biker gang. He soon discovers that his new friends are vampires and he is turning into one himself, forcing his younger brother to team up with a pair of heavily armed comic shop employees to battle the undead and save his soul. Comedy horror, starring Kiefer Sutherland, Jason Patric, Dianne Wiest, Corey Feldman and Corey Haim

programme.title: Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome (1985)
programme.desc: The nomadic warrior pitches up in the city of a cut-throat desert community in search of his stolen vehicle and ends up making a deal with a local dictator to get rid of her rival for power. However, when the bargain turns sour, he is cast out into the wilderness - where he becomes the unlikely protector of a tribe of feral youngsters. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mel Gibson, Tina Turner and Bruce Spence

programme.title: Weird Science (1985)
programme.desc: Two sexually frustrated teenagers use their computer to design the perfect woman, but are ill prepared for the outcome when their creation comes to life. She decides to make men of them, by putting them in a series of situations that will force them to stand up for themselves. John Hughes sci-fi comedy, starring Kelly LeBrock, Anthony Michael Hall, Ilan Mitchell-Smith, Robert Downey Jr and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Friendships Death (1987)
programme.desc: An android is sent to Earth by aliens on a diplomatic mission, and arrives in the middle of the the Jordanian Civil War of 1970. She forms a connection with a British reporter covering the conflict, and through him, hopes to learn to understand humanity. Sci-fi drama, starring Tilda Swinton and Bill Paterson

programme.title: The Breakfast Club (1985)
programme.desc: Five teenagers who occupy different areas of their schools social structure are thrown together for a Saturday morning detention. During the day, they analyse their behaviour and find unexpected common ground. Drama, directed by John Hughes and starring Emilio Estevez, Judd Nelson, Molly Ringwald, Anthony Michael Hall, Ally Sheedy and Paul Gleason

programme.title: E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial (1982)
programme.desc: A lonely boy befriends an alien who has been left stranded on Earth. The two develop a psychic connection as the boy helps the extraterrestrial to contact his own people and call for rescue. However, living on Earth starts to have a devastating effect on the visitors health, and a government operative is determined to capture him for study. Steven Spielbergs sci-fi fantasy, starring Henry Thomas, Drew Barrymore, Dee Wallace and Peter Coyote

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mothers heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover

programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly travels to the 21st century, where he has to save his children from a terrible fate. However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: The Naked Gun 2 1/2: The Smell of Fear (1991)
programme.desc: Blundering cop Frank Drebin discovers a secret conspiracy to kidnap a renowned solar energy expert and replace him with an imposter. As he sets out to bring the perpetrators to justice, he is unexpectedly reunited with an old flame. Slapstick comedy sequel, starring Leslie Nielsen, Priscilla Presley, George Kennedy, Richard Griffiths and OJ Simpson.

programme.title: Fanny and Alexander (1982)
programme.desc: Ingmar Bergmans Oscar-winning semi-autobiographical drama follows the lives of two children from a theatrical family in turn of the century Sweden. Their carefree lives are thrown into upheaval by the death of their father and their mothers subsequent remarriage to a puritanical minister. But refuge from their joyless new home comes in the shape of their grandmother and her lover. Starring Pernilla Allwin, Bertil Guve, Gunn Wallgren and Lena Olin

programme.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025)
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren take on one last terrifying case involving mysterious entities they must confront. Supernatural mystery drama, starring Vera Farmiga, Patrick Wilson and Elliot Cowan

programme.title: Dont Lets Go to the Dogs Tonight (2025)
programme.desc: An eight-year-old girl experiences life on her familys Rhodesian farm during the final stages of the Bush War. Drama, written, directed by and starring Embeth Davidtz, with Lexi Ventner and Zikhona Bali

programme.title: Blue Moon (2025)
programme.desc: Set over the course of one turbulent night in March 1943, Lorenz Hart, the waspish wordsmith responsible for The Lady Is a Tramp and My Funny Valentine, finds his life unravelling on the opening evening of his ex-partners show, Oklahoma! Director Richard Linklaters biographical comedy drama, starring Ethan Hawke, Margaret Qualley, Bobby Cannavale and Andrew Scott

programme.title: The Shadows Edge (2025)
programme.desc: A brilliant cyber-criminal gang evades the polices state-of-the-art surveillance system with a haul worth billions. Retired tracking expert Wong Tak-Chong is ushered back into active service to mentor rookie officer He Qiuguo and build an elite unit to stop the criminals. Action thriller, starring Jackie Chan, Zhang Zifeng and Tony Leung Ka-fai

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues (2025)
programme.desc: Nigel Tufnel, David St Hubbins and Derek Smalls reunite after a 15-year break for one final concert. Their progress is charted once more by documentary maker Marti DiBergi, who offers perhaps too much perspective on their new direction. Comedy sequel, written by and starring Michael McKean, Harry Shearer, Christopher Guest and director Rob Reiner, in his final film. Kerry Godliman and Chris Addison also star with cameos by Paul McCartney, Elton John and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Regretting You (2025)
programme.desc: A mother and daughter grapple with whats left after a devastating accident reveals a shocking betrayal and forces them to rediscover themselves. Romantic comedy drama, starring Allison Williams, Mckenna Grace and Dave Franco

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale (2025)
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal. Romantic drama, starring Joely Richardson, Joanne Froggatt and Paul Giamatti

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey (2025)
programme.desc: New York bachelor David is due to drive to his friends wedding, but a wheel clamp on his vehicle derails those plans. He acquires a new drive from the mysterious Car Rental Company managed by two nameless agents, who are oddly familiar with his dating history. At the wedding, David meets fellow singleton Sarah and squanders an opportunity to dance with her. On the way home, the rental cars GPS asks him if he would like to embark on a big bold beautiful journey. When David agrees, the virtual assistant guides him to Sarah and they follow a winding route between physical doors that open into episodes from their respective pasts. Romantic drama, starring Colin Farrell and Margot Robbie

programme.title: Wuthering Heights (2026)
programme.desc: Catherine Earnshaw is raised by her alcoholic father at a Yorkshire farmhouse, with Nelly Dean as a constant companion. Mr Earnshaw acquires an orphaned boy named Heathcliff during one of his gallivants and gifts the waif to Cathy as her pet. The children become inseparable, and as adults they embark on a destructive relationship. Writer-director Emerald Fennells drama based on Emily Brontes romance, starring Margot Robbie, Jacob Elordi and Martin Clunes

Jeffrey hides out at a toy shop for several months, and during forays outside the building, he sparks romance with store worker Leigh Wainscott. Comedy drama, starring Channing Tatum, Kirsten Dunst and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: The Murderer and Me
programme.desc: Factual crime stories. This documentary tells the story of the Home Counties mother of two who became one of Britains most notorious killers

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: The story of Britains first female agents sent to the front line during the Second World War and spymistress Vera Atkins, who led them. In the tumult of the conflict, French Resistance fighters sought aid from Britains Special Operations Executive (SOE) against Nazi occupation. Led by Romanian-born Atkins, SOE enlisted female agents including Odette Sansom and Noor Inayat Khan to take the fight to the Germans. As tensions soared, Noor navigated occupied France, while Atkins grappled with tough decisions. She and her team raced against time to safeguard SOEs mission, braving sacrifices for victory

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: Amid the chaos of D-Day, alarming wireless messages revealed Special Operations Executive (SOE) disloyalty. While concerns escalated, Allied troops advanced and Paris was liberated. SOE operations subsequently dwindled, prompting Second World War spymistress Vera Atkins to confront the harm done to her agents

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers
programme.desc: The remarkable story of retired war gamer Gilbert Roberts, officer Jean Laidlaw and the Womens Royal Navy Service (WRNS) - aka the Wrens - as they use revolutionary war gaming tactics to defeat Admiral Karl Donitz and his Wolf Pack fleets of U-boats in the Battle of the Atlantic during the Second World War. The Second World War breaks out and a force of U-boats try to starve Britain into submission. Meanwhile, Vera Laughton Mathews recruits talented women into the WRNS. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The north of England is so rich in archaeological finds that Alice Roberts travels back there once again to reveal more of its fascinating history. She and the team examine a Roman fort on Hadrians Wall, evidence of early writing by the Picts in Scotland, and a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age coffin buried under a golf course

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits more archaeological sites around the country, with digs in southern England revealing a previously unknown Roman town, a Tudor ship buried beneath a quarry and evidence of Henry VIIIs financial forgery under the Tower of London

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Arthurs Stone and a Georgian Mine
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits more archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history during the prehistoric, Roman, Saxon and Tudor eras. In this edition she heads to a spectacular monument that is older than Stonehenge, a 200-year-old mine trapped in time, and a lost medieval friary

programme.desc: The Battle of the Catalaunian Plains, fought in 451, between the Huns under Attila and a mixed Roman and Visigoth force under Aetius and Theodoric I. The terrible battle ended in a bloodbath, with no clear victory

programme.title: Clash of Ancient Warriors
programme.desc: The Battle of Sekigahara was a historical battle in Japan which was fought in 1600. It saw Tokugawa Ieyasu emerge victorious over his rival Ishida Mitsunari, uniting the country under the Tokugawa shogunate

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: The defeat at Stalingrad in 1943, which was the turning point of the Second World War and convinced the Nazi leadership to intensify their terror and propaganda campaigns

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A fresh look at the Third Reich from a global and current perspective, telling how the Nazis conquered Germany and then half of Europe within a few years of losing the First World War. The survivors and the winners from the war wanted to blame the Germans. No one knew whether the Germans had really broken with the Nazis

programme.desc: Startling evidence suggesting that the Ancient Maya conspired with aliens, as experts hypothesise that they acquired extraterrestrial knowledge of astrology

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Alien Corridors
programme.desc: There are recordings of possible UFO and UAP sightings across the world which have created patterns. This series investigates whether the Earth is being mapped by intergalactic forces. A team of multi-disciplined experts - believers and critics - investigates what these Alien Corridors are and what they mean. Washington State holds the United States highest rates of UFO sightings per capita. With a new report made every 55 hours, why are UFOs choosing to hover here?

programme.title: Ancient Skies
programme.desc: How people continued to unleash their imaginations on the skies, including wondering whether humans are truly alone in the universe

programme.title: Combat Machines
programme.desc: The stories of remarkable feats of military engineering, featuring how an American GI and his Harley motorbike became a legend

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.sub-title: The Lost Language, the Beauty Machine and the Disk of Strangeness
programme.desc: A look at perplexing and unusual objects. This episode focuses on a lost language, a beauty machine and something called the Disk of Strangeness

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: The Castle of Chambord, the largest and most iconic French castle. Designed on the medieval model of fortified castles with its enclosure and large corner towers, this jewel is inspired by the Gothic style. The ornamentation on the upper sections reaches skywards, with the chimneys. Its silhouette is distinctive, making it one of the architectural masterpieces of the Renaissance. In this royal palace of excess, every dimension is breathtaking

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate mysterious beings with big heads and eyes, whose images were found carved into rocks. Known as the greys, they are rumoured to be alien life forms

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts analyse whether natural causes were responsible for the volcanoes, earthquakes and floods of ancient times - and the possibility that it was down to aliens

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: The story of Britains first female agents sent to the front line during the Second World War and spymistress Vera Atkins, who led them. In the face of danger, Vera ramped up her recruitment of female agents. Chaos ensued as the Prosper circuit in Paris neared collapse. Meanwhile, Noor, deep in Parisian resistance, struggled to maintain cover. Vera enlisted agents like Violette Szabo and Yvonne Baseden, but uncertainty shrouds their upcoming missions

programme.title: The Lost Women Spies
programme.desc: While Europe rejoiced over victory in the Second World War, Odette Hallowes struggle at Ravensbruck revealed telling information about other agents in prison. Spymistress Vera Atkins investigation intensified in Karlsruhe prison and Ravensbruck, focusing on espionage agent Noor Inayat Khans fate

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers
programme.desc: After the fall of France in 1940, Donitzs most famous U-boat commanders embarked on a race to send British merchant ships to the bottom of the sea. Driven by a fierce competition to sink the highest tonnage, they terrorised the North Atlantic convoys and converged on them in wolfpack attacks. All the while, Vera Laughton Mathews Wrens worked around the clock to bring the battle directly to Donitzs U-boats. When confronted with unprecedented losses, and the real risk of starvation, the British Royal Navy seized on a manoeuvre developed by the famous U-boat hunter, Commander Johnny F Walker. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Headless Romans and Anglo Saxon Gold
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits more archaeological digs around the country, finding out about a gatehouse riddled with Civil War bullets, a unique Iron Age shield made from bark, and Roman burials with pots where the heads should be

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Mystery Shipwreck and a Roman Army Camp
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits more archaeological digs around the country, finding out about discoveries that shed new light on British history during the prehistoric, Roman, Saxon and Tudor eras. In this edition, she examines a Dutch ship sunk by the English, a Cornish Roman fort and a 5,000-year-old Neolithic monument

programme.title: Britains Secret Islands
programme.desc: A visit to the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, home of the 5,000-year-old standing stones of Callanish, traditional Scottish blackhouses and cut peat

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate mysterious beings with big heads and eyes, whose images were found carved into rocks. Known as the greys, they are rumoured to be alien life forms

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts analyse whether natural causes were responsible for the volcanoes, earthquakes and floods of ancient times - and the possibility that it was down to aliens

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: In the Philippines, archaeologists discover the remains of an ancient rhinoceros. Near the Columbia-Panama border, an old car is discovered in the jungle. A Brazilian island is infested with thousands of deadly snakes, while in Kenya, human remains are found deep in a cave

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: A hidden ancient city is found in Micronesia. In New Guinea, an airplane wreck is investigated. A strange glowing forest is discovered in India, while an immense tunnel system in the Brazilian jungle is explored

programme.title: Battle 360
programme.desc: CGI reconstruction of the Second World War Pacific battle of Midway, and how American aircraft carriers Enterprise, Yorktown and Hornet turned the tide in favour of the Allies

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. This episode comes from Leeds, where remains of children and teenagers have been uncovered beneath a shopping arcade. Analysis of the broken bones reveals that they date back to the industrial revolution, and a thorough examination of the remains provides a detailed picture of the lives these young people would have lived during a tumultuous period of British history

programme.title: Alien Corridors
programme.desc: Patterns in UFO and UAP sightings across the world. A strange formation is seen in the sky over the sleepy suburbs of Tinley Park, Illinois. The lights return for a second time almost to the date the following year

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: The Allies continued to push from the west to Berlin, while survivors recount the horrors behind finding Germanys concentration camps

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific nears its violent end as a drastic tactic triggers the nuclear age, and the brave men share their thoughts behind the tragic events

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate mysterious beings with big heads and eyes, whose images were found carved into rocks. Known as the greys, they are rumoured to be alien life forms

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos studies Indias Sanskrit texts, which are filled with descriptions of brain surgery, flying crafts, nuclear physics, and other advanced sciences. He learns that Hindu scholars attribute this knowledge to a time when gods walked among humans

programme.title: Hitlers Coup 1923
programme.desc: The infamous Beer Hall Putsch of November 1923, an attempt by Adolf Hitler to take power in Germany by force. Though it failed, it was a day that would change the world for ever, birthing Hitlers regime

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A fresh look at the Third Reich from a global and current perspective, telling how the Nazis conquered Germany and then half of Europe within a few years of losing the First World War. This first episode recounts how the clash of right-wing and left-wing political convictions turned the Weimar Republic into a powder keg. The fascist movement then seized power in Italy in 1922

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-host Sarah Cruddas examine the Chicago OHare International Airport case of November 2006 - the sighting of a flying saucer hovering over a United Airlines airport gate. The incident was witnessed by multiple aviation professionals, pilots and passengers. The immediate aftermath of the event was captured in real time by audio recordings of communication between the airports Air Traffic Control tower and airline staff, but the sighting was dismissed by the US governments Federal Aviation Administration as a weather phenomenon. Craig and Sarah talk to UFO investigator Robert Powell, who reveals the UFO punched a circular hole in the clouds as it departed upwards after hovering over the terminal. Craig and Sarah also learn astonishing details about how aviation authorities handled the incident

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: A look at the CIAs rumoured investment in psychological operations, asking if there could be an army of remote viewers watching the American public today

programme.desc: How many ancient architectural wonders bear similarities despite being built by different civilisations, questioning whether aliens could have designed them

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Mountain Men

programme.title: The Curse of Oak Island
programme.desc: After raising the 40,000lb boulder from Lot 8, the team is astonished to find a manmade feature that may be hiding a shaft or tunnel.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces
programme.sub-title: Shipwrecked Sailors of the Roman Empire
programme.desc: The Mediterranean Sea witnessed unprecedented maritime traffic. Many ships perished there, while carrying valuable cargo destined to extend the power of Rome. Kevin Sempe dives in search of these wrecks, without which Rome could never have existed.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces
programme.desc: Kevin Sempe recalls the events of 1992, when a deadly cloud of steam and ash churned through plantations and villages, flattening the grand city of St Pierre, in Martinique. It then thundered into the bay where it sank 18 ships and hundreds of smaller craft. Within a minute or two, nearly 32,000 people died

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Lancaster Castle has witnessed scenes of historical, cultural and political significance throughout the centuries, including incidents of religious persecution, the trials of the Lancashire Witches and 200 public executions. Owned by the Duchy of Lancaster, it was a state prison until 2011 and is still a working crown court. Dan Jones explores its turbulent history and meets Wendy Robinson, a retired prosecutor from Australia, whose ancestors were sentenced to death at Lancaster Crown Court in 1817 for stealing sheep

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Arundel Castle, in West Sussex, has been the seat of the Dukes of Norfolk and their ancestors for over 850 years, with its fortunes rising and falling with those of the Howard family. A particularly low point occured when Thomas Howard, the fourth Duke of Norfolk, was executed by Elizabeth I for allegedly plotting to marry her rival Mary Queen of Scots. Dan Jones meets a weapons expert, who shows him how Richard FitzAlan, the third Earl of Arundel, would have fought in the 100 Years War. He also meets a social historian, who gives an insight into how Arundel would have prepared for Queen Victorias visit in 1846

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: First-hand accounts and rare archive footage reveal the devastation of the German attack on France. During the Blitzkrieg, the Allies worked furiously to keep their ground

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War

programme.desc: Eye-witness accounts and archival footage describe the German U-boat push into British waters, featuring stories of bravery and new technologies employed in the conflict

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the Galapagos, a collection of volcanic islands in the Pacific known for its extraordinary wildlife. More than 150 years after Charles Darwin formulated his ground-breaking theory of evolution on the remote archipelago, Attenborough explains how life on the islands has continued to evolve in biological isolation, examining how the ever-changing volcanic landscape has given birth to species and sub-species that exist nowhere else in the world. He begins by revealing how Galapagos rose from the ocean four million years ago

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster reveals how life has learnt to adapt quickly in the ever-changing volcanic landscape, resulting in species that exist nowhere else in the world. David investigates the driving forces behind their evolution, exploring the appearance and behaviour of remarkable creatures and vegetation including giant whale sharks, marine iguanas and seemingly normal dandelion seeds that grow into plants the size of trees

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael is delighted when Todd Packer arrives at the Dunder Mifflin office and demands a desk job - but the salesmans arrogant behaviour infuriates the rest of his colleagues, and Jim and Dwight decide to get the new arrival out of their lives for good. Meanwhile, Andy is annoyed when Pam gives Erin a new computer. Sitcom, starring Steve Carell, with David Koechner (Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy)

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael decides to propose to Holly, and searches for an unforgettable way to pop the question - but Pam is forced to intervene when he tries to start a fire in the car park. The Dunder Mifflin employees hold a jumble sale in the warehouse, where Dwight tries to show off his trading skills and Kevin, Andy and Darryl play a board game based on the TV soap Dallas

programme.title: Attenborough at 100: Behind the Lens
programme.desc: As David Attenborough turns 100, this intimate film presents new interviews, behind-the-scenes footage and extraordinary clips

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Natural History Museum Alive
programme.desc: The broadcaster invites viewers to join him as he takes an after-hours stroll around one of his favourite places, the Natural History Museum. But this is no ordinary tour as some of the many exhibits spring to life as the veteran naturalist strolls along

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Following his visit to the Great Barrier Reef in 1957, naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough returns and uses the latest filming techniques to unlock the secrets of the natural wonder. In the first edition, he descends beneath the waves at night and uses cutting-edge technology to generate computer scans of the sea floor. He also learns about a complex community of animals, as well as the annual coral spawning, which is responsible for the great diversity in the reef

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest technology, the naturalist explores the shark-infested waters of Osprey Reef in the Triton submersible, before returning to the nesting grounds of the Green Sea Turtle, 60 years after his first visit to Raine Island. He also marvels at manta ray cleaning stations on Lady Elliot Island, and tracks dwarf minke whales to discover their surprising reasons for returning, and why the reef is vital for their survival

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The naturalist examines what the next few decades may hold for this remarkable ecosystem, and looks at the work of scientists who are trying to preserve the area and the species that inhabit it, assisted by research vessel the Alucia. He also embarks on an ambitious attempt to dive further into the reef than ever before to witness the resilient coral that live down in the depths and see if they hold answers that may help to overcome challenges that lie ahead, as the impact of humans on the reef continues to take its toll

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa begins to feel increasingly neglected by Homer and tries to worm her way into his affections by feigning an interest in American football. Amazingly, she soon shows an aptitude for predicting the outcome of games - an ability that sets him off on an amazing but ill-fated run of betting success

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge suffers a nervous breakdown and has to be packed off for a well-earned rest, leaving Lisa and Bart with Patty and Selma, and Homer alone with Maggie

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mrs Krabappel tires of her lonely life and searches for love in the personal ads, unaware that Bart knows of her plight and sees it as the perfect opportunity to make mischief. Meanwhile, Homer promises to stop swearing if Ned Flanders shaves off his moustache

programme.title: Chicken Run (2000)
programme.desc: A headstrong chicken and her fellow fowls ponder how to escape from a grim Yorkshire farm before the evil farmers wife can turn them into pies. Luckily, help arrives in the form of a heroic American rooster who promises to show them how to fly. Nick Parks animated comedy, with the voices of Mel Gibson, Julia Sawalha, Miranda Richardson and Jane Horrocks

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Grampa is press-ganged into babysitting Bart, Lisa and Maggie while Marge and Homer visit a rural retreat in a bid to save their ailing marriage

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Three Men and a Comic Book
programme.desc: Bart becomes convinced Milhouse and Martin are trying to steal a valuable comic book they all clubbed together to buy. As his paranoia escalates, the three enter into a dangerous confrontation that threatens to end their friendship. With the guest voice of Daniel Stern

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Mr Burns receives a life-saving blood transfusion from Bart and responds with a brief thank-you note - much to the disgust of Homer, who feels his son has not been adequately rewarded for his noble gesture

programme.desc: Will Spann is driving his soon-to-be ex-wife Lisa to her parents home when she mysteriously disappears without a trace during a stop at a gas station. Thriller, with Gerard Butler and Jaimie Alexander

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Rise of Animals
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his two-part exploration of the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years by looking at the history of mammals. He traces their journey from humble origins as tiny nocturnal forest-dwellers to their astonishing explosion in diversity and domination of the world following the death of the dinosaurs

programme.title: Flying Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the world of the pterosaur, a flying creature that occupied the skies 200 million years ago. Visiting sites around the world, from the Dorset coast to New Mexico, he explains how the reptiles evolved to defy the forces of gravity, and examines the features of their anatomy that can still be seen in present-day descendents. He also discovers why they became extinct after dominating the skies for millions of years

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Natural History Museum Alive
programme.desc: The broadcaster invites viewers to join him as he takes an after-hours stroll around one of his favourite places, the Natural History Museum. But this is no ordinary tour as some of the many exhibits spring to life as the veteran naturalist strolls along

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Global Adventure
programme.desc: Documentary capturing some of the greatest filming sequences of David Attenboroughs career, using previously unseen footage, from the deepest dives in a submersible on the Great Barrier Reef to watching a million bats take off in a cave in Borneo. Other highlights include life in the frozen tundra of South Georgia, a colony of King Penguins and the worlds largest migration of Hammerhead sharks in the Galapagos Islands

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids

programme.title: Attenborough at 100: Behind the Lens
programme.desc: As David Attenborough turns 100, this intimate film presents new interviews, behind-the-scenes footage and extraordinary clips

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Wild City
programme.desc: With the latest film-making technology, the veteran broadcaster uncovers the unseen natural treasures of Singapore and explores how the has adapted to humans
